# ajq — expanded public context

> ajq is a Go CLI for semantic jq workflows. It keeps ordinary jq execution
> byte-deterministic and makes semantic work explicit, bounded, and observable.

This checked-in context artifact is curated only from public ajq repository
material. It contains no private task, internal planning, credential, or model
provider response data. For current source material, use the public links in
the sections below.

## README and quick start

ajq is useful when literal jq filters are insufficient for JSON or NDJSON text,
but structural selection and output must remain predictable. Pure jq queries do
not initialize a backend or make model calls.

```bash
# Discover commands and safe agent workflows.
ajq --help
ajq capabilities --json
ajq examples

# Pure jq is deterministic and requires no semantic backend.
printf '{"users":[{"name":"Ada"}]}' | ajq -r '.users[].name'

# A deterministic, no-network semantic query shape check.
printf '[{"id":1,"msg":"please keep this"}]' \
  | ajq --backend mock -c '.[] | select(.msg =~ "keep") | .id'
```

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ricardocabral/ajq/main/README.md)
- [Install ajq](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/install/)
- [First deterministic pipeline](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/tutorials/first-pipeline/)

## Safe agent workflow and core execution concepts

1. Run `ajq --help` for human command discovery and `ajq capabilities --json`
   for the versioned machine-readable capability contract. Capabilities is static
   introspection: it does not load credentials, construct a backend, provision
   assets, or contact a network.
2. Run `ajq examples` for categorized copy-pasteable workflows.
3. Check a semantic query's jq shape with `--backend mock`. Mock is
   deterministic and does not use a network, cloud credentials, or local model
   assets. It cannot establish semantic quality on a production model.
4. Use representative input with `--explain` to inspect semantic call sites and
   estimated post-dedup judgements. `--explain` exits without executing against
   a configured model/provider backend.
5. Select a real backend explicitly only after review, and set a finite
   `--max-calls` cap appropriate to the task.

Semantic work is explicit in the jq query and is cache-keyed by backend, model,
operation/spec, and canonical value. The pure jq path stays byte-reproducible.

- [Agent-safe semantic workflow](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/agent-safe-semantic-workflow/)
- [Split execution](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/explanation/split-execution/)
- [Determinism contract](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/explanation/determinism/)
- [Three-phase executor](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/explanation/three-phase-executor/)

## CLI discovery, examples, and planning

`ajq capabilities --json` is the stable machine contract. Consumers should use
its `schema_version` as the contract discriminator and ignore unknown fields
and future enum values for compatible v1 builds. `ajq examples [topic]` provides
safe examples for `pure-jq`, `semantic-filter`, `explain`, `classification`,
and `ndjson`; semantic examples explicitly select `--backend mock`.

```bash
# Explain a representative semantic query before a model run.
printf '[{"msg":"refund requested"},{"msg":"profile updated"}]' \
  | ajq --explain '.[] | select(.msg =~ "refund request") | .msg'

# Explicit real backend execution: set a finite cap and observe the result.
printf '[{"msg":"refund requested"}]' \
  | ajq --backend local --max-calls 10 --stats \
      -c '.[] | select(.msg =~ "refund request") | .msg'
```

`--max-calls` caps post-dedup backend judgements; paid backends default to 100
but agents should set an explicit cap. `--stats` reports workload and cache
observations after a successful run.

- [CLI reference](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/reference/cli/)
- [`--explain` output reference](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/reference/explain-output/)
- [Estimate model calls](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/estimate-model-calls/)
- [Control semantic costs](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/control-costs/)

## Semantic functions and supported limits

The shipped semantic surfaces are fuzzy matching (`=~`, `!~`, and `sem_match`)
and bounded `sem_classify`. Use semantic filters to find JSON records by meaning
and classification to route values into fixed labels.

```bash
printf '[{"id":1,"msg":"payment failed"}]' \
  | ajq --backend mock -c '.[] | select(.msg =~ "payment failure") | {id, msg}'

printf '{"id":1,"msg":"billing question"}\n' \
  | ajq --backend mock -c '{id, route: sem_classify(.msg; "billing"; "bug"; "account")}'
```

Do not present `sem_extract` or `sem_redact` as general-purpose supported
transforms. Their unbounded value-operator contexts remain limited and fail
loudly outside documented gated/interleaved execution shapes.

- [Filter JSON by meaning](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/filter-json-by-meaning/)
- [Classify JSON and NDJSON streams](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/classify-json-streams/)
- [Semantic functions reference](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/reference/semantic-functions/)
- [Project status](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/explanation/project-status/)

## Cache, privacy, and backend safety

Successful semantic judgements are stored locally to reuse repeated work. Use
`--no-cache` when private or one-off processing must bypass both persistent
cache reads and writes; it is not a cost or call limit.

```bash
printf '[{"msg":"refund requested"}]' \
  | ajq --backend mock --no-cache --stats \
      '.[] | select(.msg =~ "refund request") | .msg'
```

Never put API keys in commands, logs, or checked-in files. Cloud credentials are
read from environment variables. Pick a backend explicitly for real semantic
work and retain a finite `--max-calls` guard. Pure jq operations need no backend.

- [Manage the judgement cache](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/how-to/manage-the-cache/)
- [Backends reference](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/reference/backends/)
- [Configuration reference](https://ricardocabral.github.io/ajq/docs/reference/configuration/)
- [Security policy](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ricardocabral/ajq/main/SECURITY.md)
